page: Vultr voc-s-4c-32gb-640s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 shared vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor includes 16 MB of L3 cache and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AES, and SHA_NI. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, and includes 640 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate a strong single-core capability, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 12814.11 and large-block memory read bandwidth reaching 86.9 GB/sec. The server exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and database operations, as evidenced by a weak rating in bzip3 multi-threaded decompression and PassMark database operations. Large LLM inference workloads also show poor to weak performance, though smaller model text generation remains average.

Given its shared CPU allocation and high memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. The combination of 32.0 GB of RAM and 640 GB of NVMe SSD storage makes it suitable for caching layers, memory-resident databases, and general-purpose applications with moderate CPU demands. It is less suited for heavy multi-threaded compute tasks, high-throughput web serving, or large-scale language model inference.
description: Vultr voc-s-4c-32gb-640s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 4 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor with 2 physical cores and a 2.0 GHz clock speed. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, offering a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, and 640 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance, though multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform at average to weak levels. Lacking a dedicated GPU, it is not designed for heavy parallel acceleration. This server is best suited for memory-intensive workloads with moderate compute requirements, such as caching systems, memory-resident databases, and general-purpose applications that benefit from a high memory-to-vCPU ratio without requiring dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-4c-32gb-640s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 640 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance for memory-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-4c-32gb-640s-amd is an AMD EPYC server with 4 shared vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 640 GB NVMe storage, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
640 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in benchmarks
Best suited for caching, memory-resident databases, and general-purpose applications
